prevalence.source: ILAE/European epilepsy epidemiology syntheses and IHME GBD estimates
prevalence.trend: stable; improved access to diagnosis and treatment reduces uncontrolled epilepsy rates

incidence.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) / European cohort studies

burden.mortalityRate: Elevated vs general population (SUDEP and injury-related)

vulnerablePopulations: people with socioeconomic barriers to specialist care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ILAE criteria; EEG and MRI widely used; epilepsy surgery workup for drug-resistant cases

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; high diagnostic access in most member states
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: ~70% seizure control with appropriate therapy; higher access to specialist escalation than many regions

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High availability of EEG/MRI and specialist epilepsy clinics in many countries
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Broad antiseizure medication formulary; structured pathways for drug-resistant epilepsy including surgery, VNS, and ketogenic therapies
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Care variability across member states and persistent psychosocial impacts despite treatment availability
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong specialist networks and access to advanced diagnostics and epilepsy surgery programs
